ORDER ON MOTION TO REQUIRE VEXATIOUS LITIGANT TO PROVIDE SECURITY PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 391.1

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BUCKLEY REAL ESTATE, INC.'S MOTION TO REQUIRE VEXATIOUS LITIGANT TO PROVIDE SECURITY PURSUANT TO CCP 391.1 IS GRANTED. MOVING DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN DECLARED A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT AND PLAINTIFF'S CASE LACKS MERIT. PLAINTIFF MUST POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $20,000 WITHIN FIFTEEN DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER BEING MAILED TO HIM. THE CASE IS STAYED PENDING THE POSTING OF THE BOND. IF THE BOND IS NOT TIMELY POSTED, MOVING DEFENDANT MAY SEEK DISMISSAL PER AN EX PARTE APPLICATION WITH THE REQUISITE NOTICE TO THE PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2012 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-23-2012 Continued To Sep-26-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Moti And Motion To Require Vexatious Litigant To Provide Security Pursuant To Ccp 391.1; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-18-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark J. Romeo, Esq. of the Law Offices of Mark J. Romeo, Tel. 415.395.9315, appearing for defendant and moving party;
Martin Eng, Tel. 415.246.1111, plaintiff and opposing party appearing on his own behalf.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BUCKLEY REAL ESTATE, INC.'s Motion To Require Vexatious Litigant To Provide Security Pursuant To CCP 391.1 is Granted. Moving defendant has shown that plaintiff has been declared a vexatious litigant and plaintiff's case lacks merit. Plaintiff must post a bond in the amount of $20,000 within fifteen days of notice of entry of the order being mailed to him. The case is stayed pending the posting of the bond. If the bond is not timely posted, moving defendant may seek dismissal per an ex parte application with the requisite notice to the plaintiff. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976
